# Moonwish Studios — From Zero to 8 Clients

## At a Glance

**Role:** Co-founder & Web Developer (Oct 2022 – June 2023)
**What I did:** Co-founded a web dev studio, shipped sites for local businesses, ran every side of the business
**Stack:** React, TypeScript, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Supabase, Figma

---

## The Story

Before I ever worked at a company, I started one. Moonwish Studios was a web development studio my co-founder and I built from scratch — no connections in the industry, no portfolio to point to, just cold outreach and the ability to ship fast.

We targeted local businesses that needed a real web presence but couldn't afford agency prices. The pitch was simple: professional sites, fast turnaround, fair pricing.

---

## What I Built

Over 8 months, we shipped **8 production websites** and generated **$5k+ in revenue**. Every project was built on the same modern stack — React, TypeScript, Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase — which let us move fast without sacrificing quality.

The biggest unlock was creating a **shared Figma design system.** Instead of designing every site from scratch, we built a library of reusable components and layout patterns that we could adapt per client. That cut our design cycles from weeks down to **3–4 days**, which meant we could take on more projects without burning out.

---

## What I Owned

Everything. I scoped projects, wrote contracts, managed timelines, handled client communication, designed in Figma, built the frontend, deployed to production, and iterated based on feedback. When something went wrong — a client changed scope mid-project, a deadline got tight — it was on me to figure it out.

This is where I learned that shipping isn't just about code. It's about understanding what a client actually needs (which is often different from what they ask for), setting expectations, and delivering something they're proud to show people.

---

---

## What I Took Away

Moonwish taught me how to own outcomes, not just tasks. When you're the founder, there's no one to escalate to — you either solve the problem or lose the client. That mindset carried directly into my work at Bluon, where I now own a product end to end with the same sense of accountability.

It also taught me that speed compounds. The design system, the reusable stack, the templated contracts — every investment in process paid off across all 8 projects. I think about this constantly when building developer tooling and infrastructure now.
